target: dense F64 cumulative scan over contiguous last-axis lines
lever: Safe-Rust direct-output cumulative scan splits independent contiguous last-axis lines across scoped threads for large multi-line tensors; small, one-line, and strided scans keep the existing in-place serial path.

baseline.command: rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- eval/cumsum

final_rebench.command: rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- eval/cumsum

isomorphism.ordering: Per output line, accumulation order is unchanged: forward scans visit increasing element index, reverse scans visit decreasing element index. Threading only partitions independent whole lines.
isomorphism.floating_point: No reassociation occurs within a line. NaN, infinity, signed-zero, and cummax/cummin edge behavior are covered by threaded_cumulative_matches_serial_reference.
isomorphism.tie_breaking: No tie-breaking state changes; cummax/cummin call the same primitive operation in the same per-line order.
isomorphism.shape_and_errors: Shape, dtype, arity, axis parsing, zero-sized fallback, and non-dense fallback remain on the existing eval_cumulative path.

golden_output.fixture: eval/cumsum_4096x1024_f64_axis1 input data[i] = i * 0.001; output bytes are little-endian f64 bit patterns from the serial per-row cumsum reference.

proof_commands: rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-lax cumulative --lib -- --nocapture
rch exec -- cargo clippy -p fj-lax --all-targets -- -D warnings
decision: Keep landed commit 5c7b5fe86490ee30555c1c0651c0b10c29d79b37. This bead duplicates the same profile-backed hotspot and is closed against that landed implementation plus this validation evidence.
next_profile_target: Long single-line cumulative scans need a Blelloch-style chunked parallel prefix primitive because line threading cannot parallelize the dependency chain.
